items.title: Anthropic Publishes Cyber Jailbreak Severity Framework with Amazon, Google, Microsoft
items.summary: Anthropic proposed a five-level Cyber Jailbreak Severity (CJS-0 to CJS-4) scale scored on four axes: capability gain, breadth of capability gain, ease of weaponization, and discoverability. The framework was co-developed with Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and other Glasswing partners and is open for feedback from academia, industry, civil society, and government. The post also detailed Fable 5's four-category cybersecurity classifier: prohibited use (blocked), high-risk dual-use (blocked), low-risk dual-use (sometimes blocked), and benign use (allowed).

quantitative_claims.claim: CJS scale scores 0-10 combining four axes each scored 0-2 or 0-4

items.conflict_of_interest: Anthropic is the primary author of the framework it is proposing as an industry standard

items.title: Together AI Raises $800M Series C to Scale Open-Model Inference Infrastructure
items.summary: $800M Series C from Aramco Ventures, NVIDIA, Vista Equity, General Catalyst, and others. Together AI serves customers including Cognition, Decagon, Eleven Labs, Cursor, and Suno with open-model inference at claimed 31% higher throughput vs next-fastest OSS engine. Annual bookings exceeded $1.15B per multiple press outlets; primary blog does not disclose valuation.

quantitative_claims.claim: 31% more TPS than next-fastest OSS engine (production coding agent workloads)
Decagon reduced inference costs 6x after switching to Together AI

items.caveats: 31% TPS claim is vendor-measured benchmark. Savings claims in press release (6x-20x) inconsistent with primary blog. Annual bookings figure from TechCrunch, not primary blog.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches DSX AI Factory Revenue-Share Program; Partners Deploy 210,000 GPUs in Australia and Indonesia
items.summary: NVIDIA launched a new model allowing AI cloud companies to acquire GPU infrastructure through a revenue-sharing and credit-support arrangement, earning NVIDIA both hardware margin and a share of cloud service revenue. Initial named partners: Sharon AI (up to 40,000 GB300 GPUs, 72 MW, Australia) and Firmus/DayOne (up to 170,000 GPUs, 360 MW Batam, Indonesia — expected $25-30B committed offtake over 6 years).

quantitative_claims.claim: Sharon AI: up to 40,000 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs, 72 MW
Firmus/DayOne: up to 170,000 GPUs, 360 MW, Batam, Indonesia
Firmus expects $25-30B committed offtake over 6 years

items.caveats: GPU counts are target/max figures. Circular financing criticism noted: NVIDIA separately holds large stakes in OpenAI ($30B) and xAI ($20B). Firmus Indonesia deal was first reported by Bloomberg June 28 (outside strict window); NVIDIA consolidated blog July 1 is primary.

items.title: TwelveLabs Raises $100M Series B and Signs AWS Trainium Exclusivity Deal
items.summary: $100M Series B led by NEA and NAVER Ventures with Amazon, Radical Ventures, and Index Ventures. TwelveLabs builds video cognition infrastructure combining Marengo (video embedding model) and Pegasus (video-language model). A multiyear AWS Trainium agreement commits new models to launch first on AWS.
items.caveats: 'World's most powerful video embedding model' is vendor-claimed, no independent benchmark cited. '90% of world's data is video' is a widely-cited industry estimate, not sourced specifically. No performance benchmarks for Marengo 3.0 or Pegasus 1.5 from independent testers.
